figures.caption: Table 1. Comparison of image-level and pixel-level detection performance and reasoning performance under zero-shot settings. Detection results are reported as (AUROC, ACC), and reasoning results as (ROUGE-L, SBERT). The column ‘Anno.’ denotes the supervision type used in training, where ‘I’ represents image-level supervision, ‘P’ denotes pixel-level annotations, and ‘T’ indicates task-specific instruction-tuning data designed for anomaly detection. * indicates anomaly maps are generated with ReAL.
Figure 3. Qualitative comparison of Qwen2.5-VL-7B, Qwen2.5VL-7B+R1, and Qwen2.5-VL-7B+CGRO.
Figure 6. Comparison of GPT-4.1, Triad, AnomalyGPT, EIAD, and our method.

Figure 1. Visualization of token-to-patch attention maps for certain reasoning tokens. Only a small subset of tokens (highlighted in red) exhibits focused attention on the true anomaly regions (red boxes), and these tokens are also semantically related to anomaly concepts (e.g., “scratch”, “mark”).
Figure 5. Qualitative comparison of Qwen2.5-VL-7B, Qwen2.5-VL-7B+R1, and Qwen2.5-VL-7B+CGRO.

Figure 4. Visualization of different token selection used in ReAL. Each column shows anomaly maps generated by: (a) keeping all tokens (b) filtering with spatial entropy SI, (c) filtering with semantic relevance ST, (d) combining both scores with weighted aggregation. ST measures semantic alignment with anomaly-related concepts, while SI measures attention concentration. Methods that incorporate both criteria yield more accurate anomaly maps.

Figure 2. Overview of the proposed reasoning-driven anomaly detection framework. Given an input image, the MLLM generates a reasoning process and a final anomaly answer. The ReAL module selects anomaly-related tokens based on semantic relevance ST and spatial entropy SI, then aggregates their visual attentions to obtain a pixel-level anomaly map. During training, these selected tokens are further used by the CGRO module to leverage reinforcement learning jointly driven by a reasoning–localization consistency reward and R1-based accuracy and format rewards, enabling the model to align reasoning tokens with their corresponding visual attentions while simultaneously improving reasoning correctness, structural quality, and anomaly localization accuracy.
